page: Google Cloud Platform a2-ultragpu-2g is an accelerator-optimized server configured with two NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs providing a total of 160 GB VRAM. The system features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon CPU with 24 dedicated vCPUs (12 physical cores) operating at 2.2 GHz. It is equipped with 340 GB of system memory, translating to 14.17 GB per core, and includes 804 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.

Benchmark data reveals a clear performance profile. Single-core CPU performance is relatively weak, as shown by stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores in the bottom 25%. However, multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall into the average tier. The server's primary strength lies in its GPU-accelerated capabilities, achieving top-tier performance in large language model inference tasks. Specifically, prompt processing for medium and large models reaches top-tier benchmarks, with text generation for a 70B model achieving 24.17 tokens per second.

The hardware profile represents a high-density resource allocation where CPU-bound single-threaded tasks are deprioritized in favor of massive parallel processing. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, this server is optimized for workloads that can fully leverage its dual A100 GPUs and large VRAM capacity, making general-purpose CPU computing less cost-effective on this platform. It is highly suited for deep learning, large language model inference, and GPU-accelerated data science workloads, while being less optimal for single-threaded CPU tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform a2-ultragpu-2g is an accelerator-optimized server designed for parallel computing workloads. It features two NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs with 160 GB of total VRAM, paired with an Intel Xeon x86_64 CPU offering 24 dedicated vCPUs (12 physical cores) running at 2.2 GHz. The system is configured with 340 GB of RAM and 804 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. While single-core CPU benchmarks place this server in the lower tier, its multi-core capabilities and memory bandwidth are average, and its GPU-driven LLM inference performance is in the top tier. This server is highly efficient for deep learning, large language model inference, and GPU-accelerated data science, though it presents a tradeoff with weaker single-threaded CPU performance.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform a2-ultragpu-2g is an accelerator-optimized server featuring 2 NVIDIA A100 GPUs (160GB VRAM), 24 vCPUs, 340GB RAM, and 804GB local NVMe SSD storage for high-end AI workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform a2-ultragpu-2g features 2 NVIDIA A100 GPUs, 24 vCPUs, 340GB RAM, and 804GB NVMe SSD for GPU-accelerated workloads.
tagline: An accelerator-optimized server featuring dual NVIDIA A100 GPUs and 340 GB RAM for high-throughput parallel computing and LLM inference.
bullet_points: Equipped with two NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs providing 160 GB of total VRAM
Features 24 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 340 GB of system memory
Includes 804 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Delivers top-tier performance for large language model inference and prompt processing
Best suited for deep learning, data science, and GPU-accelerated parallel workloads
